Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief 
Conflict of Interest Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
April 5, 1977 
Re: 
Statement of Economic Interest, 
Legal or Brokerage Service 
Dear Mr. Spurlock: 
Please find enclosed a copy of my memorandum of February 23, 1977 
to the City Council of the City of Lakewood which is self-
planatory. 
On pages 3 and 4 I have marked portions of that 
memorandum which gave me trouble. 
I have not been able to find 
in your loose leaf service an opinion on this subject matter. 
Perhaps you have rendered an opinion. 
If not, you may wish to 
do so. 
The issue arises as to an attorney, and as to a real estate 
broker, both city officers, and both having a place of business 
within the City of Lakewood. 
If the word "source" in §82030 
has reference to the client or person who paid the fee or 
commission, then the fee of that person would not have to be 
reported if that person does not do business within the city, 
who is not planning to do business within the city, or has not 
done business within the city for two years preceding. 
On the 
other hand if the word "source" as used in §82030 is considered 
to be the business enterprise engaged in by the filer, providing 
legal or brokerage service, the application of §82030 is 
relatively simple. 
If this latter view is taken and the filer 
does not have a legal or brokerage office within the city, and 
is not planning to do any business within the city, or has not 
done business within the city during the preceding two years, 
reporting would be completely avoided. 


Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief 
April 5, 1977 
Page Two 
I am disturbed in that I do not believe Proposition 9 was 
intended to grant the broad exclusion that the logical 
application of the 1976 amendment to §82030 would indicate. 
Your advice or assistance would be appreciated. 
JST:ft 
Enclosure 


· 
MEMORANDUM 
February 23, 1977 
TO: 
CITY COUNCIL 
CITY ADMINISTRATOR 
COPIES: 
CITY CLERK 
FROM: 
CITY ATTORNEY 
SUBJECT: 
OFFICERS OF THE CITY -
STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST 
This is a reminder of my memorandum of January 28, 1977 wherein 
I advised you that your Statement of Economic Interest, pursuant 
to Government Code §87203 must be filed on or before April 1, 
1977. 
In this memorandum I set forth Fair Political Practices 
Commission forms that you should use. 
We note in reviewing the Instruction Manual prepared by the 
Fair Political Practices Commission pertaining to Schedule 721.D 
at page 6. (Income Aggregating $250.00 or more), a statement 
appears that you must disclose income aggregating $250.00 or 
more from anyone source during the reporting period. 
In 1976 
§82030 of the Government Code pertaining to the d~finition of 
"income" was amended by adding the following provisions: 
"Income, other than a gift, does not include income 
received from any source outside the jurisdiction and not 
doing business within the jurisdiction (city), not 
planning to do business within the jurisdiction, or not 
having done business within the jurisdiction during the 
two years prior to the time any statement or other action 
is required under this Title." 
Therefore, if the source of income is from a source located out-
side the City of Lakewood, and one of the following exists, 
the same need not be reported: 
1. 
The source does not do business within the city, and is 
not planning to do business within the citYi or 
2. 
The source has not done business within the city during 
two years prior to the December 31st preceding the filing 
of the statement. 
As we previously advised you in the memorandum of April 19, 1976, 
when income is required to be reported the Statement shall include 
under §87207(a) the following: 
1. 
The name and address of each source of income aggregating 
$250.00 or more in value, or $25.00 or more in value if 
the income was a gift, and a general description of the 
business activity, if any, of each source. 
2. 
A statement whether the aggregate value of income from each 
source was greater than $1,000.00 or $10,000.00. 


Memorandum to City Council and City Administrator 
February 23, 1977 
Subject: 
Officers of the City -
Statement of Economic Interest 
3. 
A description of the consideration, if any, for which the 
income was received. 
4. 
In the case of" a gift, the amount and date on which the gift 
was received. 
This item is covered in the Instruction Manual 721 on Schedule 
721-D. 
You will note that what you report in general is: 
1. 
The name and address of the source. 
2. 
An indication of whether the amount received was greater or 
less than $1,000.00, or greater or less than $10,000.00, 
or over $10,000.00, but you do not set forth the specific 
amount of income. 
Obviously, if by reason of the afore-
mentioned amendment to §82030 "income" is from a source 
outside of the jurisdiction (within the aforementioned 
categories), the same need not be reported. 
In addition to the requirement of §87207, if you are engaged in 
providing legal or brokerage services, such as an attorney, 
insurance brokerage, real estate brokerage, or stock brokerage, 
you must complete Schedule 721-F, in accordance with Instruction 
Manual 721 at page 6. 
In this regard §87207(b) of the Government 
Code provides that when income of a business entity, including 
income of a sole proprietorship, is required to be ~eported under 
this Article, 
the statement shall contain: 
1. 
The name and address, and general description of the business 
activity of the business entity. 
2. 
In the case of a business entity which provides legal or 
brokerage service, the name of every person who paid fees 
to the business entity, if the filer's pro rata share of 
fees from such person was equal to or greater than $1,000.00. 
3. 
In the case of a business entity not covered by paragraph 2 
above, the name of every person from whom the business 
entity received payment, if the filer's pro rata share of 
gross receipts from such person was equal to or greater 
than $10,000.00 during a calendar year. 
This latter category 
is reported on Schedule 721-G. 
We have not located any opinion or administrative action of the 
Fair Political Practices Commission clarifying the application 
of the 1976 amendment to §87207 (b). ' We note immediately a 
conflict between the language of the two sections as follows: 
1. 
Under §82030 excluded from the definition of "income" is 
income from: 
A. 
A source that does not do business wi t-hi n 
t-ho r";.j.-,. 


~ 
Memorandum to City Council and City Administrator 
February 23, 1977 
Subject: 
Officers of the City -
Statement of Economic Interest 
B. 
A source that has not done business within the City 
during two years prior to December 31 preceding the 
filing of the statement. 
2. 
Section 87207 pertains to the statement reporting income 
and the contents thereof, and provides as follows: 
A. 
B. 
Under subsection (a) "when income is required to be 
reported" the same shall be reported as therein set 
forth. 
This section then has reference to reporting 
the source of income such as shoe store business, 
stock dividends, bank accounts, etc., and does not 
require specific enumeration of clients or persons 
paying the income. 
The reference to "when income 
is required to be reported" is clearly to §82030, 
which defines "income" and therefore the exclusion 
from that definition contained in §82030, as amended 
in 1976, is applicable. 
Under subsection (b) when "income of a business entity, 
including income of a sole proprietorship, is require.E_ 
to be reported under this Article, the statement shall 
contain." 
In this particular category we have the 
additional provisions that where the business entity 
provides legal or brokerage service the statement must 
also contain the name of every person who pays fees 
to the business entity, if the filer's pro rata share 
-
7-
of fees from such person is equal to or greater than 
$1,000.00. 
Again, however, in this section it is only 
the income required to be reported, and therefore 
reference is made to §82030. 
The question arises, 
therefore, if the source is one not doing business 
within the city or planning to do business within the 
city, or a a source that has not done business within 
the city during two years prior to December 31st, 
whether or not the name of that client or customer needs 
to be reported. 
I have not located any opinion or administrative action of the 
Fair Political Practices Commission clarifying the foregoing. 
It appears that the two sections create an ambiguity that is not 
readily resolved: 
1. 
If the word "source" in §82030 has reference to the client 
or person who paid the fee, then that fee and the person who 
paid it would not be reported if that person does not do 
business within the city, or is not planning to do business 
within the city, or has not done business within the city 
for two years preceding. 
As stated previously, the determin-
ation of what is "doing business" and whether one intends 
to do business or plans to do business is very difficult. 


Memorandum to City Council and City Administrator 
February 23, 1977 
Subject: 
Officers of the City -
Statement of Economic Interest 
2 . 
If the "source" is considered to be the business enterprise 
engaged in by the filer providing legal or brokerage services, 
the application of §82030 is relatively easy. 
The effect, 
however, might be to afford a broad exclusion not intended by 
the amendment. 
If the latter viewpoint is utilized the filer 
who does not have a law office or brokerage agency within the 
city, and is not planning to do any business within the city, 
of has not done business within the city during the preceding 
two years, would not have to report any income. 
Again we 
still have the difficult problem of what is "doing business" or 
"planning to do business". 
I do not believe that Proposition 9 as adopted by the people 
intended to grant the broad exclusion that the logical application 
of the aforementioned sections would indicate in paragraph 2. 
Not being sure of the result of these two sections, it would be 
my recommendation in reporting income under §87207(b) that all 
fees be reported from a legal or brokerage service engaged in by 
the filer, if the filer's pro rata share of fees from such person 
are equal to or greater than $1,000.00. 
I would also recommend 
that a clarifying opinion be requested from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
JST:ft

sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Lakewood seeks FPPC clarification on whether the term 'source' in Government Code §82030 (regarding income exclusion) refers to the client/payer or the filer's business entity, particularly for attorneys and real estate brokers filing economic interest statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document consists of a cover letter requesting advice (dated April 5, 1977) followed by the City Attorney's February 23, 1977 memorandum to City Council that prompted the request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The actual advisory question is embedded in the narrative discussion of the two statutory provisions.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Lakewood submits a memorandum requesting FPPC guidance on interpreting §82030 and §87207(b) regarding income reporting requirements for city officers who are attorneys and real estate brokers, specifically questioning whether income from clients/customers located outside the city must be reported.
